description: Issue #205 Cat 3: Improve CJK vertical writing mode accuracy

userStories.title: Improve CJK vertical writing accuracy with better CID→Unicode and vmtx parsing
userStories.description: pdfjs/vertical.pdf (~61% chars) and pdfbox/pdfbox-3127-vfont-reduced.pdf (~13% chars) pass their current thresholds but are far from 100%. The remaining accuracy gaps come from: 1) Missing CID→Unicode mappings for vertical glyphs, 2) Missing TrueType vmtx (vertical metrics) table parsing for precise vertical positioning.
acceptanceCriteria: TrueType vmtx table is parsed for vertical advance/origin metrics
Vertical CID→Unicode mappings use improved CID tables (from US-205-1)
No regression on horizontal CJK PDFs (cjk_mixed.pdf stays at F1=1.0)
userStories.notes: Key files: crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/cid_font.rs (W2/DW2), crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/interpreter.rs. This story depends on US-205-1 (CID tables) for better CID→Unicode resolution. TrueType vmtx table format: vMetrics[numOfLongVerMetrics] with (u16 advanceHeight, i16 topSideBearing), then i16 topSideBearing entries. Need 'vhea' table for numOfLongVerMetrics. vmtx is optional in TrueType - only parse if present. For CIDFonts, W2 and DW2 arrays should take precedence over vmtx when available.
